name: MRSA
notes: PBP2a-mediated resistance to all beta-lactams except ceftaroline. Stratify CA vs HA — CA-MRSA usually retains susceptibility to TMP-SMX/clinda/doxy.

name: MSSA
notes: Beta-lactams (nafcillin, oxacillin, cefazolin) are MORE effective than vancomycin for MSSA — switch as soon as susceptibility known.

name: VRSA
notes: Extremely rare. ID consult mandatory. vanA gene transfer from VRE.

name: VISA
notes: MIC 4-8 mcg/mL. Suspect with persistent bacteremia despite vancomycin. Switch to daptomycin (high-dose) or ceftaroline.

name: Coagulase-negative Staph
notes: Usually contaminant on blood culture. Treat if true infection (line, prosthetic). S. lugdunensis is more virulent — treat like S. aureus.

name: Strep pneumoniae
notes: PCN-resistant rate ~30% US. Empiric meningitis dosing assumes PCN-resistance until known. Vaccines: PCV20/PCV21/PPSV23.

name: Group A Strep (S. pyogenes)
notes: Universally penicillin-susceptible. Add clindamycin for toxin suppression in necrotizing/severe disease.

name: Group B Strep (S. agalactiae)
notes: Intrapartum prophylaxis if colonized. Universally PCN-susceptible.

name: Viridans strep
notes: Native valve IE classic pathogen. PCN MIC matters for IE duration.

name: Strep anginosus group
notes: Strong association with abscess formation. Source control + extended duration.

name: Enterococcus faecalis
notes: Ampicillin-susceptible majority. IE: ampicillin + ceftriaxone (Sanford preferred) OR ampicillin + gentamicin.

name: Enterococcus faecium
notes: Often ampicillin-resistant; many are VRE. Check VRE status before empiric coverage.

name: VRE
notes: Daptomycin (high-dose 10-12 mg/kg) for bacteremia; linezolid alternative. Cystitis: nitrofurantoin or fosfomycin if susceptible.

name: Listeria monocytogenes
notes: Add ampicillin to empiric meningitis regimen for age >50, immunocompromised, pregnant. Cephalosporins do NOT cover.

name: Corynebacterium spp
notes: Often contaminant. C. jeikeium and C. urealyticum often resistant — treat with vancomycin if true infection.

name: Bacillus cereus
notes: Most blood culture isolates are contaminants. True infection in IVDU or post-trauma.

name: B. anthracis (anthrax)
notes: Reportable. Contact CDC. Cipro or doxy first-line.

name: Neisseria meningitidis
notes: Reportable. Close-contact prophylaxis. Vaccine for serogroups A/C/W/Y and B.

name: Neisseria gonorrhoeae
notes: CDC 2021 STI guideline: ceftriaxone 500mg IM x1 (1g if >150kg). Zoliflodacin oral approved 2025 as alternative.

name: Moraxella catarrhalis
notes: Almost all beta-lactamase positive — use beta-lactam/inhibitor or 2nd/3rd-gen cephalosporin.

name: H. influenzae
notes: Hib vaccine has dramatically reduced invasive disease. Beta-lactamase ~30% — empirically use BL/BLI or 3rd-gen ceph.

name: E. coli
notes: Local antibiogram critical. ESBL prevalence rising — ~15% in US outpatient UTI in 2025.

name: ESBL Enterobacterales
notes: IDSA 2024 AMR Guidance v3.0: cystitis — nitrofurantoin/TMP-SMX/cipro/levo if susceptible. Pyelo/cUTI — TMP-SMX/cipro/levo preferred; ertapenem/meropenem if needed. BSI — meropenem.

name: Klebsiella pneumoniae
notes: Hypervirulent K1/K2 strains cause invasive liver abscess syndrome. KPC carbapenemase common in healthcare.

name: CRE
notes: IDSA 2024 v3.0: KPC — meropenem-vaborbactam, ceftazidime-avibactam, imipenem-relebactam. NDM/MBL — ceftazidime-avibactam + aztreonam OR cefiderocol OR aztreonam-avibactam (Emblaveo, 2025). OXA-48 — ceftazidime-avibactam.

name: Enterobacter spp
notes: AmpC inducible. IDSA 2024 v3.0 lists in 'AmpC' group with C. freundii, S. marcescens, M. morganii, P. aeruginosa, P. rettgeri. Avoid 3rd-gen cephs even if susceptible — risk of derepression. Use cefepime, carbapenem, or pip-tazo (with caution).

name: Citrobacter freundii complex
notes: AmpC group — see Enterobacter notes. C. koseri does NOT carry AmpC.

name: Serratia marcescens
notes: AmpC group. Intrinsically resistant to colistin.

name: Morganella morganii
notes: AmpC group. Intrinsically resistant to nitrofurantoin, polymyxins, tetracyclines.

name: Proteus mirabilis
notes: Intrinsically resistant to nitrofurantoin, tetracyclines, polymyxins. Indole-positive Proteus (P. vulgaris, P. penneri) carry inducible cephalosporinase.

name: Providencia spp
notes: AmpC group (P. rettgeri). Intrinsically resistant to nitrofurantoin, polymyxins, tigecycline.

name: Salmonella (non-typhi)
notes: Most diarrhea is self-limited and antibiotics not indicated (prolong shedding). Treat invasive disease, immunocompromised, age extremes.

name: Salmonella Typhi
notes: Reportable. Travel history. XDR strains in Pakistan — IDSA recommends carbapenem or azithromycin empirically.

name: Shigella spp
notes: Reportable. Increasing fluoroquinolone and azithromycin resistance, especially in MSM outbreaks. CDC notes XDR strains 2022-2025.

name: Campylobacter jejuni
notes: Treat severe disease, prolonged symptoms, immunocompromised. Macrolide preferred (FQ resistance >40% in US).

name: Yersinia enterocolitica
notes: Iron-overload states (hemochromatosis, transfusion) predispose. TMP-SMX or FQ.

name: Pseudomonas aeruginosa
notes: Always check antibiogram. Empiric anti-pseudomonal options: cefepime, pip-tazo, meropenem, ceftolozane-tazobactam (DTR), ceftazidime-avibactam, cefiderocol. Consider 2-drug therapy in severe disease pending susceptibilities.

name: DTR Pseudomonas
notes: IDSA 2024 v3.0: ceftolozane-tazobactam preferred. Alternatives: ceftazidime-avibactam, imipenem-relebactam, cefiderocol. Avoid colistin/polymyxin B as monotherapy.

name: Stenotrophomonas maltophilia
notes: Intrinsically resistant to carbapenems. IDSA 2024 v3.0: TMP-SMX + minocycline or TMP-SMX + cefiderocol; minocycline-tigecycline alternative.

name: Acinetobacter baumannii
notes: MDR/XDR common. IDSA 2024 v3.0: sulbactam-durlobactam (Xacduro, 2023) preferred for CRAB. Alternatives: high-dose ampicillin-sulbactam (12g/d sulbactam component), polymyxin B, minocycline, cefiderocol.

name: Burkholderia cepacia complex
notes: CF lung infection. Intrinsically resistant to many drugs. TMP-SMX, meropenem, minocycline, ceftazidime per susceptibility.

name: Vibrio vulnificus
notes: Cirrhosis + raw oysters or saltwater wound = high mortality. Doxycycline + ceftriaxone IDSA SSTI 2014.

name: Legionella pneumophila
notes: Atypical CAP coverage required. Levofloxacin or azithromycin first-line.

name: Mycoplasma pneumoniae
notes: Macrolide resistance now common (~10-30% US). Doxycycline alternative.

name: Chlamydia pneumoniae
notes: Atypical CAP. Macrolide or doxycycline.

name: Chlamydia trachomatis
notes: CDC 2021 STI guideline: doxycycline 100mg PO BID x7d preferred (was azithromycin 1g x1).

name: Treponema pallidum (syphilis)
notes: Bicillin LA for early/late latent. Aqueous PCN G for neurosyphilis. PCN allergy in pregnancy = desensitize.

name: Borrelia burgdorferi (Lyme)
notes: Doxycycline 100mg BID x10-14d for early Lyme (now safe in young children per AAP).

name: Oral anaerobes
notes: Amoxicillin-clavulanate or ampicillin-sulbactam first-line. Clindamycin alternative but C. diff risk.

name: Bacteroides fragilis group
notes: Metronidazole, BL/BLI, carbapenems. Cephalosporins (except cefoxitin) do NOT cover.

name: Clostridioides difficile
notes: IDSA 2021 update: fidaxomicin preferred over vancomycin for both initial and recurrent CDI. Bezlotoxumab for recurrence prevention. FMT for refractory.

name: Clostridium perfringens
notes: Penicillin + clindamycin (toxin suppression). Surgical debridement essential.

name: Actinomyces spp
notes: Long course penicillin (months). Sulfur granules.

name: Nocardia spp
notes: Acid-fast weakly. TMP-SMX backbone, often with imipenem ± amikacin for severe disease. Long course.

name: M. tuberculosis
notes: ATS/CDC/IDSA guideline. RIPE (rifampin/isoniazid/pyrazinamide/ethambutol) x2 mo, then RI x4 mo. MDR/XDR — refer to TB specialist; bedaquiline/pretomanid/linezolid (BPaL) regimen.

name: MAC / NTM
notes: ATS/IDSA NTM 2020 guideline. Macrolide-based 3-drug regimen for MAC pulmonary; expert ID consult for M. abscessus.

name: Candida albicans
notes: Echinocandin first-line for candidemia per IDSA 2016 (update pending). Step-down to fluconazole if susceptible and stable.

name: Candida glabrata (Nakaseomyces)
notes: Often azole-resistant or dose-dependent. Echinocandin preferred. Increasing echinocandin resistance — check MIC.

name: Candida auris
notes: CDC urgent threat. Echinocandin first-line. Resistance to fluconazole common; some pan-resistant.

name: Aspergillus spp
notes: Voriconazole or isavuconazole first-line per IDSA 2016 aspergillosis. Posaconazole alternative. Triazole resistance increasing.

name: Cryptococcus neoformans
notes: Induction: liposomal amphoB + flucytosine; consolidation/maintenance fluconazole. WHO 2022 update single high-dose ambisome regimen.

name: Pneumocystis jirovecii (PCP)
notes: TMP-SMX 15-20 mg/kg/d TMP component divided. Add steroids if PaO2 <70 or A-a >35.

name: Histoplasma capsulatum
notes: Endemic Ohio/Mississippi River valleys. Mild — observation; moderate-severe — itraconazole; severe/disseminated — liposomal amphoB then itra.

name: Coccidioides
notes: Endemic SW US. Most resolve spontaneously. Fluconazole high-dose for moderate-severe and meningitis (lifelong).

name: Blastomyces
notes: Itraconazole; amphoB for severe/CNS.

name: Mucorales (mucormycosis)
notes: Surgical debridement essential. Liposomal amphoB; isavuconazole alternative.

name: HSV
notes: Acyclovir/valacyclovir/famciclovir. HSV encephalitis IV acyclovir 10 mg/kg q8h x 14-21d.

name: VZV
notes: Valacyclovir 1g TID x 7d for shingles. Higher dose acyclovir for VZV vs HSV.

name: CMV
notes: Ganciclovir/valganciclovir; foscarnet for resistant. Letermovir prophylaxis HSCT.

name: Influenza
notes: Oseltamivir within 48h ideal; baloxavir single dose alternative.

name: RSV
notes: Supportive. Nirsevimab/palivizumab for high-risk infants. Ribavirin for HSCT/lung Tx — controversial.

name: SARS-CoV-2
notes: IDSA COVID guideline (living). Outpatient — nirmatrelvir-ritonavir if high-risk. Hospitalized — remdesivir + dexamethasone if hypoxic.

name: HIV
notes: DHHS guidelines. ART regimen selection — beyond v1 scope; refer to specialist.

name: Helicobacter pylori
notes: ACG 2024 guideline: bismuth quadruple therapy preferred (PPI + bismuth + tetracycline + metronidazole) over clarithromycin triple (resistance >15%).

name: Sarcoptes scabiei
notes: Permethrin 5% topical or oral ivermectin 200 mcg/kg x2 (day 0 and 14). Crusted scabies — combination.

name: Giardia lamblia
notes: Tinidazole single dose preferred over metronidazole.

name: Plasmodium spp (malaria)
notes: CDC malaria guideline. Severe — IV artesunate. Uncomplicated — atovaquone-proguanil or artemether-lumefantrine.

name: Toxoplasma gondii
notes: Pyrimethamine + sulfadiazine + leucovorin. TMP-SMX alternative.
